next_steps: Send comprehensive autoimmune/vasculitis panel including ANA, ANCA, RF, anti-CCP, complement levels (C3, C4), and cryoglobulins
Perform electrodiagnostic-guided sural nerve biopsy and concurrent muscle biopsy for vasculitis pathology
Obtain high-quality MRI of cervical and lumbar spine with contrast under sedation or analgesia to rule out structural/inflammatory cord involvement
Evaluate for underlying occult malignancy given the rapid functional decline and systemic inflammation
Monitor response to high-dose vitamin B6 supplementation while awaiting diagnostic biopsy results
Consult rheumatology and neurology for multidisciplinary management of suspected vasculitis
